meta_title: For Founders: Git with AI to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Git with AI to Solve no project ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to use Git with AI to overcome the 'no project ideas' block. An intermediate case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch SaaS MVPs.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for inspiration; manufacture it. By leveraging Git repositories as training data for Claude Code and using Antigravity for rapid scaffolding, you can transform existing open-source patterns into unique, monetizable SaaS MVPs. This workflow automates the ideation phase, turning technical exploration into immediate, deployable product architecture.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You have the technical skills to build, but you are paralyzed by the 'what'—the elusive hunt for a project that is both technically challenging and commercially viable. Most founders wait for a lightning bolt of inspiration that never comes, while the market moves on without them.

But what if you didn't need to invent? What if you could synthesize existing successful patterns into a new, high-value product? By integrating Git-based AI workflows with rapid scaffolding, you stop being a dreamer and start being a founder who ships. This is how you bypass the 'no project ideas' trap and move straight to revenue.

steps.title: Analyze High-Velocity Git Repos
steps.description: Identify a domain, then clone a high-star repository to analyze its architecture. We want to extract core logic, not copy the UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the codebase structure of this repository. Identify the 3 most complex core business logic modules. Explain how they handle state management and data persistence in a way that could be abstracted for a different industry vertical.

steps.title: Define the Pivot Opportunity
steps.description: Use the AI to find the 'missing feature' or 'niche vertical' that the original repo ignores but users are begging for in the Issues tab.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scan the GitHub issues for this project. Identify the top 3 requested features that were ignored or deemed 'out of scope'. Propose a new SaaS MVP architecture that solves these specific pain points using the core engine we analyzed.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Based on the issue tracker, what is the most requested feature that would make this a standalone B2B SaaS?'

steps.title: Scaffold the MVP Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate code based on your new, pivoted requirements rather than the original repo's bloat.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a scaffold for a new SaaS MVP based on [Project Name] core logic, but refactored for [Target Niche]. Include a clean database schema, authentication, and a stripped-down API layer using Next.js and Supabase.

steps.title: Implement Domain-Specific Logic
steps.description: Inject the unique value proposition that separates your tool from the original project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the specific business logic for [Unique Feature] that integrates with the base scaffold. Ensure it is modular, tested, and follows the existing repository patterns for consistency.

steps.title: Automate Documentation and Deployment
steps.description: Generate professional documentation and a deployment-ready configuration to get to market in hours, not weeks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a README.md, a landing page copy in markdown, and a deployment script for Vercel. Focus on B2B value propositions and professional SaaS branding.

pro_tips: Never clone a repo to build it; clone it to treat it as a database of architectural solutions.
Use Claude Code's 'read' command on the 'Issues' tab of a project to find the exact market demand the original author failed to capture.
Always refactor the AI-generated code to remove unnecessary dependencies; bloated MVPs kill conversion rates.
Target 'boring' B2B niches where the existing open-source tools are too technical for non-developer stakeholders.
Use Git commit history as a roadmap for your own development; if a feature took 50 commits to perfect, treat it as a high-risk area for your MVP.
resources: GitHub API for Market Research: https://docs.github.com/en/rest
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is scaffolded, deploy to a staging environment and spend exactly 48 hours getting your first 10 beta users. Do not add features until you have a signed LOI or a paid subscription.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for the sake of code and start building for the sake of business. You have the tools, the strategy, and the roadmap. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
